payload.text:
เพิ่มฟังค์ชั่นให้เวลากดหรือคลิ๊กที่ เมนู
นิยาย
การ์ตูน
หนังสือเรียน
ภาษา
ของสะสม

ให้เปิดหน้าใหม่ขึ้นพร้อมแสดง ข้อมูล หรือ คลิ๊กแล้ว เลื่อนไปยัง ข้อมูลในหน้าแรก ตามส่วนข้อมูลที่ถูกต้อง

**Examining Navigation Options**

I'm currently focused on the navigation menu's functionality: ensuring a seamless user experience when clicking options like "นิยาย," "การ์ตูน," and others. My main goal now is deciding whether each selection will launch a separate page or smoothly scroll to the relevant section. I'm leaning towards a system that intuitively connects the menu items to specific content areas.


**Considering Scrolling Solutions**

My next step is implementing smooth scrolling in `App.tsx` linked to the navigation menu in `Header.tsx`. I'll assign unique IDs to the `ProductSection` and `CategorySection` components. I've ruled out new page navigation, favoring the requested "scrolling to correct info". The current single-page design lends itself to this world-class user-friendly solution, providing a much quicker and better experience.


**Developing Anchor Links**

My attention has shifted toward section anchors within `App.tsx` for smooth scrolling functionality. I'm focusing on adding unique IDs to `ProductSection` and `CategorySection`, then linking the `Header.tsx` menu items to these anchor points. Smooth scrolling is the key to creating a world-class user experience, replacing separate page navigation. I also considered and discarded implementing a "Category View" state, in favor of my new anchor based plan.


**Crafting Dynamic Categories**

I'm now integrating category-specific anchors into `App.tsx`. I'll use IDs like `#fiction`, `#manga`, etc., and replace the mixed `ProductSection` display with a dynamically updated "Browse by Category" area, or just link to existing sections. This aims for a streamlined experience, responsive to user choices. The "category view" is officially ditched in favor of this direct approach, it is more elegant.


**Focusing On Filtering Logic**

I'm now integrating filtering logic into `App.tsx` and focusing on smooth scrolling. Navigation in `Header.tsx` will trigger a state update in `App.tsx`, where I'll filter product sections based on the selected category. The menu links will be converted to interactive elements, ensuring the page smoothly scrolls to the relevant section. I will also make sure to add IDs to the `ProductSection` component for smooth anchoring.


**Integrating Category Filters**

I'm now implementing category filtering. The menu interaction will update the `activeCategory` state in `App.tsx`, driving the filtering logic.  I've decided on smooth scrolling via `window.scrollTo` to specific anchor IDs.  I'm focusing on "fiction," "manga," and "textbook" categories, with corresponding IDs to make a sleek transition to each section. The goal is a responsive, app-like filtering experience, and a much better user experience.


**Adding New Categories**

I'm now integrating the "เรียน" category with its subcategories: "ภาษา" and "ของสะสม". I'm refining `Header.tsx` to handle these new navigation options and updating `App.tsx` to filter the product section according to the selected category. The "Category View" state is back, but just to change the filter/search title. I'm focusing on smooth scrolling within the single-page structure to improve the user experience. I'm also ensuring a clean transition and highlighted category tags.

**Connecting Category Bubbles**

I'm now integrating category bubbles in `CategorySection` to link to product section filters. `Header.tsx` will pass the category to `App.tsx` via `onSelectCategory` callback. All product data will be stored in a master list and filtered to display appropriate products, which improves performance. Using a `useRef` or `id` for the Catalog section, I'll allow a smooth scroll on category selection. I'm focusing on "นิยาย," "การ์ตูน," "หนังสือเรียน," "ภาษา," and "ของสะสม" to match the user's prompt, refining the list of categories.

I will update the application to include a dynamic filtering and smooth-scrolling system. 

### Specifications:

1.  **Navigation Logic**: 
    *   Update the `Header` component to accept a callback function for category selection.
    *   When a menu item (นิยาย, การ์ตูน, etc.) is clicked, the app will smoothly scroll to the "Catalog" section.
    *   The "Catalog" section will dynamically filter products based on the selected category.

2.  **Behavioral Updates**:
    *   **Smooth Scroll**: Use native CSS smooth scrolling and a programmatic `scrollIntoView` for cross-browser reliability.
    *   **Filtering**: If "นิยาย" is clicked, only products matching that category will appear in the main product area.
    *   **Visual Feedback**: The section title will change to reflect the active category (e.g., "รายการหนังสือ: นิยาย").
    *   **Reset**: Added a "Show All" or "X" button to clear filters and see everything again.

3.  **Visual Appearance**:
    *   Smooth transitions between filtered states.
    *   Added a "Category Badge" to the product section header to indicate the current view.
    *   Enhanced the `CategorySection` bubbles to also trigger the same filtering logic.

entries.description: Added category selection logic and click handlers to navigation menu items.
Added id and onClearFilter props to facilitate anchoring and UX.
Modified CategorySection to support selection callbacks.
Implemented filtering state, smooth scrolling, and category logic.
